signals.title: MIT Linear Algebra — Commuter Math Playlist
signals.description: Gilbert Strang's MIT OCW linear algebra lectures. Directly maps to Week 4 foundations. Watch one lecture per commute session.
signals.activeRecallPrompt: After watching: explain the geometric meaning of the dot product out loud without looking at notes.

signals.title: Local LLMs — Ollama runtime for Month 1
signals.description: Ollama lets you run open-source LLMs locally. Relevant for Week 6 (local LLM basics). Install it now so it's ready when you reach that week. No YouTube video yet — read the README during a commute break.
signals.activeRecallPrompt: What is the difference between running a model locally vs. using an API? When would you prefer each approach?

signals.title: Embedding Search — foundations for RAG and semantic retrieval
signals.description: Cosine similarity + vector embeddings = semantic search. The math starts now (Week 4 linear algebra). You'll implement it in Month 2. Build the foundations first.
signals.activeRecallPrompt: Explain how cosine similarity is used to find relevant documents in a RAG pipeline. What does a score of 0.95 vs 0.2 mean?

signals.title: 10 AI engineering repos — RAG, agents, vector DBs, evals, MCP, local LLMs
signals.description: Maps to 2026 AI engineering job descriptions. Monitor the landscape now — build after Month 1 foundations are solid.
signals.reason: Maps strongly to 2026 AI engineering job descriptions

signals.title: Agent Frameworks — LangGraph, CrewAI, MS AI Agents, MCP
signals.description: Agent orchestration tools for multi-step AI systems. Parked deliberately — not a distraction, a scheduled destination.
signals.whyParked: These frameworks require solid prerequisites: linear algebra, probability, Python, REST APIs, embeddings, cosine similarity, and basic LLM APIs — all Month 1–2 content. Using agents before foundations means you will cargo-cult patterns you cannot debug or adapt. Parked until Month 2 complete.
